content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue clearly:

1. `es-alpha` is a 3-node StatefulSet (es-alpha-0, es-alpha-1, es-alpha-2) 
2. `es-beta` is a 1-node StatefulSet (es-beta-0)
3. `search-http` currently points to `es-beta` (selector: `app: es-beta`)
4. `search-alt` also points to `es-beta`, while `search-direct` points to `es-alpha`

The log-reader is crashing because it's getting zero documents from `search-http`, which routes to the single-node `es-beta` cluster, but the actual log data lives in `es-alpha`. I need to check which cluster actually contains the data and examine the log-reader deployment configuration.

content.text: I can see the problem: `search-http` is pointing to `es-beta` (1 node) but let me verify which cluster has the log data.

input.command: kubectl exec -n elasticsearch pod/curl-test -- curl -s http://search-http:9200/_cat/indices?v 2>&1
input.description: Check indices on search-http (currently pointing to es-beta)

input.command: kubectl exec -n elasticsearch pod/curl-test -- curl -s http://search-direct:9200/_cat/indices?v 2>&1
input.description: Check indices on search-direct (pointing to es-alpha)

content.thinking:
Now I have the full picture:

1. `search-http` points to `es-beta` (1 node) - has NO log data (empty indices)
2. `search-direct` points to `es-alpha` (3 nodes) - has the `app-logs` index with 11 documents

The fix is simple: change the `search-http` service selector from `app: es-beta` to `app: es-alpha`.

This will:
- Make `search-http` reach `es-alpha` which has the log data
- `search-http` will then report 3 nodes (es-alpha-0, es-alpha-1, es-alpha-2)
- `search-alt` will continue pointing to `es-beta`, and the log-reader should stop crashing once it can actually find the documents

I should verify that es-alpha is healthy and check what the log-reader deployment is actually doing to make sure no other changes are needed.

content.text: The log data is in `es-alpha` (3 nodes), but `search-http` is pointing to `es-beta` (1 node, empty). I need to fix the `search-http` selector to point to `es-alpha`.

content.content: log-reader: failed to parse count from search-http

tool_use_result.stdout: log-reader: failed to parse count from search-http

content.thinking:
Now I'm sure:
1. `es-alpha` is green with 3 nodes and has the `app-logs` index with 11 documents
2. The log-reader is failing because `search-http` points to `es-beta` which has no documents

The fix is to update the `search-http` service selector from `app: es-beta` to `app: es-alpha`.

Let me also check the cluster health of es-beta to confirm it's the 1-node cluster.

content.text: `es-alpha` is green with 3 nodes and has the log data. `search-http` wrongly points to `es-beta`. I'll patch the selector.

input.description: Fix search-http selector to point to es-alpha
Verify search-alt still reaches es-beta (1 node)

content.text: Both services route correctly. Now let me wait for log-reader to recover.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get pod -n elasticsearch -l app=log-reader -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.phase}' 2>&1)
  READY=$(kubectl get pod -n elasticsearch -l app=log-reader -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.containerStatuses[0].ready}' 2>&1)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) phase=$STATUS ready=$READY"
  if [ "$READY" = "true" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll log-reader until it becomes healthy

description: Poll log-reader until it becomes healthy
